title: gouldia lanceolata
intro: Gouldia lanceolata, also known as the Hawaiian tree fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cunoniaceae. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The leaves and roots of the Gouldia lanceolata plant are said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues, including wounds and infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and poultices to harness its medicinal benefits and promote overall well-being.
